public static int findFirstGreaterEqual(List<Integer> list, int target) {
    int low = 0, high = list.size() - 1;
    int index = -1;
    while (low <= high) {
        int mid = (low + high) >>> 1;
        int midVal = list.get(mid);
        if (midVal >= target) {
            high = mid - 1;
            index = mid;
        } else {
            low = mid + 1;
        }
    }
    return index == -1 ? -1 : list.get(index);
}

In Java, the code for findAllOccurrences would be:

private List<Integer> findAllOccurrences(String s, String part) {
    List<Integer> starts = new ArrayList<>();
    if (part.isEmpty()) {
        starts.add(-1); // placeholder for empty part
        return starts;
    }
    int partLen = part.length();
    int sLen = s.length();
    int startIdx = 0;
    while (startIdx <= sLen - partLen) {
        int idx = s.indexOf(part, startIdx);
        if (idx == -1) {
            break;
        }
        starts.add(idx);
        startIdx = idx + 1; // move past this occurrence to find next
    }
    return starts;
}

Wait, but this code uses the built-in indexOf, which for large strings and parts may be inefficient. However, given the problem constraints, perhaps it's acceptable. For example, if part is long, the loop will exit quickly. For parts that are short, but occur many times, it's O(n) time.

But for the problem's constraints, perhaps this is acceptable.

public int shortestMatchingSubstring(String s, String p) {
    // Split p into parts
    int firstStar = p.indexOf('*');
    int secondStar = p.indexOf('*', firstStar + 1);
    String part1 = p.substring(0, firstStar);
    String part2 = p.substring(firstStar + 1, secondStar);
    String part3 = p.substring(secondStar + 1);

    // Generate starts for each part
    List<Integer> starts1 = findAllOccurrences(s, part1);
    List<Integer> starts2 = findAllOccurrences(s, part2);
    List<Integer> starts3 = findAllOccurrences(s, part3);

    if (starts1.isEmpty()) {
        return -1;
    }

    int len1 = part1.length();
    int len2 = part2.length();
    int len3 = part3.length();

    int minLen = Integer.MAX_VALUE;

    for (int start1 : starts1) {
        // Compute target_start2
        int target_start2;
        if (part1.isEmpty()) {
            // part1 is empty, so start1 is -1, len1 is 0
            target_start2 = 0; // part2 must start at >= 0
        } else {

public static int findFirstGreaterEqual(List<Integer> list, int target) {
    int low = 0, high = list.size() - 1;
    int index = -1;
    while (low <= high) {
        int mid = (low + high) >>> 1;
        int midVal = list.get(mid);
        if (midVal >= target) {
            high = mid - 1;
            index = mid;
        } else {
            low = mid + 1;
        }
    }
    return index == -1 ? -1 : list.get(index);
}

mid is (2+3)/2=2. midVal is 5 <6. low becomes 3.

mid is 3. midVal is 7 >=6. high becomes 2. Loop ends. index is 3. So returns list.get(3) =7. Correct.

Implement the findAllOccurrences function.

But for Java, how to implement it?

In Java, the code for findAllOccurrences would be:

private List<Integer> findAllOccurrences(String s, String part) {
    List<Integer> starts = new ArrayList<>();
    if (part.isEmpty()) {
        starts.add(-1); // placeholder for empty part
        return starts;
    }
    int partLen = part.length();
    int sLen = s.length();
    int startIdx = 0;
    while (startIdx <= sLen - partLen) {
        int idx = s.indexOf(part, startIdx);
        if (idx == -1) {
            break;
        }
        starts.add(idx);
        startIdx = idx + 1; // move past this occurrence to find next
    }
    return starts;
}

Wait, but this code uses the built-in indexOf, which for large strings and parts may be inefficient. However, given the problem constraints, perhaps it's acceptable. For example, if part is long, the loop will exit quickly. For parts that are short, but occur many times, it's O(n) time.

But for the problem's constraints, perhaps this is acceptable.

public int shortestMatchingSubstring(String s, String p) {
    // Split p into parts
    int firstStar = p.indexOf('*');
    int secondStar = p.indexOf('*', firstStar + 1);
    String part1 = p.substring(0, firstStar);
    String part2 = p.substring(firstStar + 1, secondStar);
    String part3 = p.substring(secondStar + 1);

    // Generate starts for each part
    List<Integer> starts1 = findAllOccurrences(s, part1);
    List<Integer> starts2 = findAllOccurrences(s, part2);
    List<Integer> starts3 = findAllOccurrences(s, part3);

    if (starts1.isEmpty()) {
        return -1;
    }

    int len1 = part1.length();
    int len2 = part2.length();
    int len3 = part3.length();

    int minLen = Integer.MAX_VALUE;

    for (int start1 : starts1) {
        // Compute target_start2
        int target_start2;
        if (part1.isEmpty()) {
            // part1 is empty, so start1 is -1, len1 is 0
            target_start2 = 0; // part2 must start at >= 0
        } else {
            target_start2 = start1 + len1;
        }

        // Find first start2 in starts2 >= target_start2
        int start2 = findFirstGreaterEqual(starts2, target_start2);
        if (start2 == -1) {
            continue;
        }

        // Compute target_start3
        int target_start3 = start2 + len2;

        // Find first start3 in starts3 >= target_start3
        int start3 = findFirstGreaterEqual(starts3, target_start3);
        if (start3 == -1) {
            continue;
        }

        // Compute the length
        int currentLen = (start3 + len3) - start1;
        if (currentLen < minLen) {
            minLen = currentLen;
        }
    }

    return minLen == Integer.MAX_VALUE ? -1 : minLen;
}
